nodes.label: Resume Manager
nodes.rationale: First uploaded resume auto-activates for UX; no hard delete in MVP for safety and version history. First manager with file handling, serving as foundation for future file-based managers.

nodes.label: Resumes Public Storage Bucket
nodes.rationale: The resumes bucket is public because resumes are meant to be publicly downloadable, avoiding signed URL complexity and latency. Paths follow {portfolioSlug}/{timestamp}-{sanitized-filename}: the slug folder enforces portfolio-scoped storage policies, the timestamp prevents collisions and gives natural version history, and filename sanitization prevents URL/path-traversal issues.

nodes.label: One Active Resume Partial Index
nodes.rationale: A partial unique index on (portfolio_id) WHERE is_active = true enforces at the database level that only one resume is active per portfolio, catching race conditions and application bugs that app-level logic alone would miss.
